shortDescription.text: Sandbox bypass or privilege escalation vulnerability.

fullDescription.text: Identifies instances where sandboxed execution environments can be bypassed, leading to unintended privilege escalation or access to host-side resources. This can allow an attacker to escape the intended security boundaries of the application.

message.text: Verification confirmed a critical sandbox bypass vulnerability related to the 'OpenClaw ACP runtime path' utilizing `sessions_spawn(runtime="acp")` functionality, as described in the skill's explanation. Threat Intel from GitHub Advisory GHSA-474h-prjg-mmw3 (OpenClaw: Sandboxed sessions_spawn(runtime="acp") bypassed sandbox inheritance and allowed host ACP initialization) directly refutes the initial benign classification. This advisory indicates that sandboxed `sessions_spawn(runtime="acp")` could bypass sandbox inheritance and initialize host-side ACP runtime, leading to improper privilege management. The initial analysis identified no suspicious lines; however, the vulnerability is inherent to the described design pattern of the skill, affecting its core functionality.

snippet.text: N/A - specific snippet not available without source code. The vulnerability is conceptual to the skill's described functionality involving `sessions_spawn(runtime="acp")`.
